ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device (e.g., S1, PC1) */,
  "device_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device type (e.g., Switch, PC, Router) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device (e.g., Data Center 1, Office 2) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description or notes about the device */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Device vendor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Device model */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Device serial number */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Device operating system */,
  "last_update_date" TEXT /* Last date of device configuration update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_device_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_type_id") REFERENCES device_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type (e.g., Switch, PC, Router) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interfaces" (
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interface */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the interface belongs to */,
  "interface_name" TEXT /* Name of the interface (e.g., VLAN 99, NIC) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address assigned to the interface */,
  "subnet_mask" TEXT /* Subnet mask for the interface */,
  "default_gateway" TEXT /* Default gateway for the interface */,
  "interface_type" TEXT /* Type of the interface (e.g., FastEthernet, GigabitEthernet) */,
  "vlan_id" INTEGER /* VLAN ID of the interface */,
  "bandwidth" INTEGER /* Bandwidth of the interface in Mbps */,
  "last_update_date" TEXT /* Last date of interface configuration update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interface_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interfaces_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role (e.g., admin, technician, auditor) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user password */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Last date of user login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., admin, technician, auditor) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the device or interface */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device being accessed */,
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interface being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., view, modify, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_interface_id FOREIGN KEY ("interface_id") REFERENCES interfaces ("interface_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration change */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the change was made to */,
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interface the change was made to */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_changes_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_changes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_changes_interface_id FOREIGN KEY ("interface_id") REFERENCES interfaces ("interface_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., online, offline, warning) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Date and time of the last status check */,
  "health_score" INTEGER /* Health score of the device (0-100) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_status_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical status entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the device at a specific point in time */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the status entry */,
  "health_score" INTEGER /* Health score of the device at the specific point in time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_history_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the note is about */,
  "note_date" TEXT /* Date the note was added */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text of the note or comment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who added the note */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_notes_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_notes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "config_backup" (
  "backup_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration backup */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the backup is for */,
  "backup_date" TEXT /* Date the backup was created */,
  "config_data" TEXT /* Backup configuration data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("backup_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_backup_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)
